This feature interacted with plenty of moisture and resulted in strong storms and very heavy rain.  Isolated supercells produced large hail and damaging winds in Runnels and Coleman counties on October 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A Trained Spotter reported quarter size hail and wind gusts of 50 to 60 mph on U.S. Highway 84 near Novice.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure system remained park across Baja California through October 17.  This feature interacted with plenty of moisture and resulted in periods of very heavy rain from October 15 through the 17.  ||Widespread flooding developed across the area and many lakes and rivers rose above flood stage and resulted in widespread flooding.  Millions of dollars of damage was done to roads and county infrastructure.  ||Johnson Fork Creek rose high enough that it caused some water to overflow onto one of the lanes on Interstate 10.  Interstate 10 had to be closed.  The Clear Fork on the Brazos River near Fort Griffin rose to 36.18 feet on October 15; The Llano River at Mason rose to 32.89 foot on October 16; Colorado River near San Saba rose to 32.53 feet on October 17; Colorado River near Ballinger rose to 22.97 feet on October 17; and the San Saba River at San Saba rose to 28.54 inches on October 16.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Prolonged heavy rainfall flooded roads across Nolan County and this flooding damaged  some roads and bridges in the county.  This damage and flooding was exasperated by all of the rainfall since September.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Mason County Sheriff's office reported Beaver Creek flowing onto a road near U.S. Highway 87, 10 miles southeast of Mason. U.S. Highway 87 south of Mason was closed. In addition, Texas Highway 71 in northeastern Mason County was closed due to flooding.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The sheriff's office reported that Johnson Fork Creek was flowing over one of the lanes on Interstate 10, with multiple swift water rescues in progress. Interstate 10 was closed as well as FM 2169 north of Interstate 10 due to the Johnson Fork Creek flooding.  First responders launched swift water rescue boats from Interstate 10 and rescued some people from their roofs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Upper level disturbances interacted with plenty of gulf moisture to produce rounds of very heavy rain.  In fact, very heavy rainfall moved north into Kimble County from Edwards County, up the South Llano River watershed area during the early morning hours of Monday, October 8.  ||About eight to 12 inches of rain fell on already saturated ground, causing major flooding over the banks of Cedar Creek and the South Llano River.  A ranging flash flood on the South Llano flooded out an RV Park located on the South Llano River in Junction, drowning four campers.  The water level at the RV Park on the South Llano River was estimated at around 21 feet.  Earlier in the morning, the South Llano River at telegraph had risen to 35 feet before the gauge failed.  The gauge reading at the Llano River near Junction measured 31.53 feet on October 8.||Additional heavy rain fell across West Central Texas and resulted in flooding and flash flooding across the area.  The following counties received a Presidential Disaster Declaration for the September through October flooding:  Brown, Callahan, Edwards, Haskell, Jones, Kimble, Mason, Nolan, San Saba, Shackelord, Sutton, Taylor and Throckmorton.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Throckmorton Sheriff's office reported flooding on the south side of town. The city park is flooded, the creek is full and a business park is moving merchandise out of the building as a precaution.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: TxDOT in Brownwood reports FM 1311 closed at State Highway 190 and State Highway 29 due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm and moist air mass lifted north from the gulf ahead of a cold front. Scattered storms developed during the afternoon with a couple becoming severe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple streets were flooded during the storm with some impassable. At least 1 vehicle was stalled on Taft AVE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm and moist air mass lifted north from the gulf ahead of a cold front. Scattered storms developed during the afternoon with a couple becoming severe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized hail was reported in Bevil Oaks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A warm and moist air mass lifted north from the gulf ahead of a cold front. Scattered storms developed during the afternoon with a couple becoming severe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Pictures from social media indicated a landspout briefly occurred in a rice field 2 miles south of China. No damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front moved across the coast during the 25th with strong north winds lingering into the 26th. The wind pushed the tide below -1 foot MLLW during the low tide on the 26th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The tide at Sabine Pass fell to -1.27 foot MLLW as strong north winds moved across the coast.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level disturbance interacted with a weak front and plenty of low level moisture to produce a cyclic supercell thundestorm during the early morning hours.  This cyclic supercell thunderstorm produced a tornado near Christoval and another one in Brady.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: National Weather Service storm survey team found evidence of damage confirming an EFO tornado in Brady.  Several trees lost limbs.  One tree was uprooted, while another tree fell on a home.  Some roof damage was also observed.

Prolonged heavy rainfall flooded roads across Haskell County and this flooding damaged some roads in the county.  This damage and flooding was exasperated by all of the rainfall since September.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a construction worker who died from electrocution and blunt force trauma.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A roofer was installing metal paneling to a commercial building under construction. Lightning struck the employee, resulting in electrocution and blunt force head trauma based on OSHA report.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Clustering storms focused along an inland-moving warm front. These storms produced localized flooding around Galveston County and Galveston Island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were reports of water near homes with flooded cars and roadways in Santa Fe, Dickinson and League City. Some of the flooding occurred as Dickinson Bayou went out of banks at SH 3 near Hughes Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging wind gusts and one tornado occurred in association with the passage of a squall line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds resulted in a flipped over RV trailer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging wind gusts and one tornado occurred in association with the passage of a squall line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There were multiple video accounts of a well-formed tornado moving through a field a few miles southeast of Eagle Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging wind gusts and one tornado occurred in association with the passage of a squall line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The EF-0 tornado tracked through Angleton and damaged firehouse bay doors while producing significant tree damage to the southeast of the firehouse. There was also minor roof damage to some surrounding structures.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging wind gusts and one tornado occurred in association with the passage of a squall line of thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An  EF-1 tornado touched down and caused damage within the Lanai Subdivision along Interstate 10 between FM 3180 and FM 565. Several homes sustained minor roof damage and numerous trees were uprooted. There were some snapped trees and power lines. One large trailer was overturned.
